refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. The FR does not contain multiple obligations requiring splitting.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency level supports safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a real-time data compression and decompression mechanism to reduce transmission time
Establish a prioritized data routing and scheduling system to minimize delays
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication latency
Use an end-to-end latency monitoring and adaptive adjustment system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a prioritized data routing and scheduling system to minimize delays

potential_sources: Interdependence between communication protocol and data compression mechanisms
Interaction between predictive control and latency monitoring systems
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on logical data flow prioritization and scheduling, which can be implemented independently of specific communication protocols or compression methods, supporting modularity and reducing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not impose additional constraints that increase latency
Predictive control algorithms are not selected as primary DP to avoid complexity and coupling risks
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interface requirements between data routing and other latency mitigation mechanisms to ensure modular integration and maintain independence.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing networked data handling and routing across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data flow for monitoring and control data

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and scheduling information for task execution

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connectivity interface

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as data routing and scheduling logically belong here; minimal diffusion as interfaces are well defined.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on physical operation; routing logic would diffuse into other modules.
Partial containment as teleoperation needs some scheduling but not full routing system; diffusion likely.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety, not data routing.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for the prioritized data routing and scheduling system because it specializes in networked data handling, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation for scheduling tasks may cause interface complexity.
If routing logic requires vehicle state data, cross-module dependencies may increase.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate routing logic from task prioritization to better align with module responsibilities and reduce cross-module diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP to establish a prioritized data routing and scheduling system logically addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency ensuring safe remote operation. The DP focuses on minimizing delays, which directly impacts latency and safety, aligning well with the FR and acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module for scheduling tasks may cause interface complexity and diffusion risk between DP and IM layers.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 8) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating good architectural packaging of the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary. However, some diffusion risk exists due to partial scheduling responsibilities in the Teleoperation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the NGHE Solution module's telemetry and network interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link) supporting the realization of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly separate routing logic from task prioritization to better align with module responsibilities and reduce cross-module diffusion, especially with the Teleoperation module.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed, the recommended revisions suggest refining the DP to explicitly separate routing logic from task prioritization to reduce diffusion risks and improve alignment with module responsibilities. Given the high safety criticality, addressing these concerns before proceeding ensures a more robust and clear decomposition.

revise.why: To explicitly separate routing logic from task prioritization, reducing cross-module diffusion risks and improving clarity of module responsibilities, especially between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently addresses the FR but can be improved for architectural clarity.
Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are adequate for current DP and IM mapping.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity and diffusion risk between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules due to overlapping scheduling tasks.
evidence_needed: Refined DP specification showing clear separation of routing and prioritization logic.
Updated containment and diffusion scores post-refinement to confirm risk mitigation.
